quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 1 Quiz 2
quiz.subtitle: Segment Addition Postulate and Segment Measures (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: Which of the following best defines a segment?
options: A set of points extending infinitely in one direction
A set of points extending infinitely in both directions
A set of points with two endpoints and all points between them
A set of points with exactly one endpoint and all points in one direction
questions.explanation: A segment is part of a line that consists of two endpoints and all points between them.

questions.question: Point $B$ lies on segment $AC$. If $AB = 3x - 5$, $BC = x + 11$, and $AC = 30$, what is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: By the Segment Addition Postulate: $AB + BC = AC$. Substituting: $(3x - 5) + (x + 11) = 30$. Simplifying: $4x + 6 = 30$, so $4x = 24$, giving $x = 6$.

questions.question: Points $A$, $B$, and $C$ are collinear with $B$ between $A$ and $C$. If $AB = 12$ cm and $BC = 19$ cm, what is $AC$?
questions.explanation: By the Segment Addition Postulate: $AB + BC = AC$. Substituting: $12 + 19 = 31$ cm.

questions.question: Point $M$ is the midpoint of segment $RT$. If $RM = 5x - 4$ and $MT = 3x + 8$, what is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: Since $M$ is the midpoint, $RM = MT$. So $5x - 4 = 3x + 8$. Solving: $2x = 12$, so $x = 6$.

questions.question: Points $P$, $Q$, and $R$ are collinear with $Q$ between $P$ and $R$. If $PR = 45$ and $QR = 18$, what is $PQ$?
questions.explanation: By the Segment Addition Postulate: $PQ + QR = PR$. Substituting: $PQ + 18 = 45$, so $PQ = 45 - 18 = 27$.

questions.question: Which notation correctly represents a ray starting at point $J$ and passing through point $K$?
questions.explanation: $\overrightarrow{JK}$ represents a ray with endpoint $J$ passing through $K$, extending infinitely in that direction.

questions.question: Point $B$ lies on segment $AD$, and point $C$ lies on segment $BD$. If $AB = 8$, $BC = 5$, and $CD = 11$, what is $AD$?
questions.explanation: The points are arranged $A$—$B$—$C$—$D$. By the Segment Addition Postulate: $AD = AB + BC + CD = 8 + 5 + 11 = 24$.

questions.question: Segments $AB$ and $CD$ have the same length. Which term correctly describes this relationship?
questions.explanation: Segments with equal length are called congruent segments, denoted by $AB \cong CD$.

questions.question: On a number line, point $X$ is at $-7$ and point $Y$ is at $5$. What is the length of segment $XY$?
questions.explanation: The length of a segment is the absolute value of the difference of the coordinates: $|5 - (-7)| = |5 + 7| = |12| = 12$ units. Length is always positive.

questions.question: Points $E$, $F$, and $G$ are collinear. If $EF = 10$ and $EG = 25$, which of the following is NOT a possible value for $FG$?
questions.explanation: If $F$ is between $E$ and $G$: $FG = EG - EF = 25 - 10 = 15$. If $E$ is between $F$ and $G$: $FG = EF + EG = 10 + 25 = 35$. These are the only two possibilities for collinear points, so $40$ is not possible.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the Segment Addition Postulate and can apply it to a variety of problems.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the basics but review midpoint problems and collinear point arrangements with multiple segments.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: Segment Addition Postulate (part + part = whole), midpoint (two equal halves), and absolute value for length on number lines.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Remember: length is always positive. If $B$ is between $A$ and $C$, then $AB + BC = AC$. A ray has one endpoint, a segment has two.
